generated_exposure.exposure_text: As someone who values both security and civil liberties, I've been following the work of Dr. Elena Vasquez, a former NATO strategic analyst. She recently pointed out that while alliances are important, over-commitment can actually weaken our national security by spreading our resources too thin. 'Smart deterrence requires focused investment,' she argues. Given current global uncertainties, maybe it's time to reassess our burden-sharing approach. What do you think?
